Contact Us

Tentative Rulings - Unified Family Court

If all parties agree to the Court's Tentative Ruling, that ruling will become the order of the Court.

If any party wishes to appear for oral argument, that party must do all of the following:
  1. Telephone the other parties or their attorneys by 4 p.m. the day before the scheduled hearing date. It is not necessary to speak directly with counsel or the other parties; it is alright to leave a message.
  2. Then, immediately advise the Court that you will appear by leaving a message no later than 4 p.m. the day before the scheduled hearing. Please call the department in which you are scheduled to appear, please leave a message if the clerk does not answer. For Department 403 please call (415) 551-3741. For Department 404 please call (415) 551-3744.
  3. If you are protected by a restraining order or if the other party is protected by a restraining order, do not contact the other party to tell him or her that you intend to appear at the hearing. You only need to contact the Court. Note: You are permitted to contact an attorney representing the other party.

PLEASE NOTE: If proper notification of all parties and the Court is not made by the 4 p.m. deadline, the Judge may not allow oral argument and the Tentative Ruling may become the order of the Court.

To Obtain Tentative Rulings Online.

Tentative rulings are available by 2 p.m. by clicking below on the link corresponding to the hearing date. The tentative rulings include all Family Law departments with matters on law and motion calendars for the following day. The date of each ruling is listed at the top of each file. Be sure to verify the date when viewing the rulings.

PLEASE NOTE: Family law cases that are designated as confidential by California law or by Court order will not be posted online. Please call (415) 551-3637 to obtain information regarding your tentative ruling.
